Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Initial Scan & Clustering by Patient Demographics: Lets look at the patient_age and patient_gender columns to see if they repeat. Event 1: 45, M Event 2: 62, F Event 3: 28, M Event 4: 45, M Event 5: 62, F Event 6: 28, M Event 7: 45, M Event 8: 62, F Event 9: 28, M Event 10: 62, F Event 11: 62, F Event 12: 45, M Event 13: 28, M Event 14: 62, F Event 15: 45, M Event 16: 28, M Event 17: 62, F Event 18: 28, M Observation: There are exactly three distinct patient profiles: 1. Profile A: 45 years old, Male (M) 2. Profile B: 62 years old, Female (F) 3. Profile C: 28 years old, Male (M)

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis: There are 3 distinct cases based on these demographics. Lets verify if the activities and departments align logically for each profile.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Detailed Analysis of Each Profile: Profile A (45, M): Event 1: 08:05:13 - Register (ER) Event 4: 08:10:50 - Triage (ER) Event 7: 08:15:42 - X-ray (Radiology) Event 12: 08:23:10 - Doctor Consult (ER) - Diagnosis: hairline_fracture Event 15: 08:30:05 - Discharged (ER) - Followup: outpatient_clinic Flow Check: Registration -> Triage -> X-ray -> Consult -> Discharge. This is a standard ER acute care flow. Time Check: Sequential and logical (08:05 -> 08:07 -> 08:15 -> 08:23 -> 08:30). Conclusion: Events 1, 4, 7, 12, 15 belong to Case A.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Profile B (62, F): Event 2: 08:07:02 - Register (ER) Event 5: 08:12:29 - Triage (ER) Event 8: 08:16:10 - Lab test requested (ER) Event 10: 08:20:27 - Lab test completed (Lab) Event 11: 08:22:54 - Doctor Consult (ER) - Diagnosis: minor_infection Event 14: 08:27:00 - Prescription created (ER) Event 17: 08:35:29 - Discharged (ER) - Followup: pharmacy_pickup Flow Check: Registration -> Triage -> Lab Request -> Lab Complete -> Consult -> Prescription -> Discharge. This is a standard ER acute care flow involving diagnostics. Time Check: Sequential and logical (08:07 -> 08:12 -> 08:16 -> 08:20 -> 08:22 -> 08:27 -> 08:35). Conclusion: Events 2, 5, 8, 10, 11, 14, 17 belong to Case B.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Profile C (28, M): Event 3: 08:07:15 - Register (Surgery) Event 6: 08:13:00 - Doctor Consult (Surgery) - Reason: scheduled_knee_surgery Event 9: 08:16:45 - Surgery preparation (Surgery) - Prep: general_anesthesia Event 13: 08:25:19 - Surgery performed (Surgery) - Procedure: arthroscopy Event 16: 08:31:37 - Recovery room (Surgery) - Condition: stable Event 18: 08:40:13 - Discharged (Surgery) - Followup: physiotherapy_sessions Flow Check: Registration -> Consult -> Prep -> Surgery -> Recovery -> Discharge. This is a standard Elective Surgery flow. Time Check: Sequential and logical (08:07 -> 08:13 -> 08:16 -> 08:25 -> 08:31 -> 08:40). Conclusion: Events 3, 6, 9, 13, 16, 18 belong to Case C.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Verification of Logic: Demographics: All events for a specific case share the exact same patient_age and patient_gender. Departments: Case A (45M) stays mostly in ER/Radiology (Acute). Case B (62F) stays mostly in ER/Lab (Acute). Case C (28M) stays mostly in Surgery (Elective). Resources: Case A uses Dr. Lee for consult. Case B uses Dr. Smith for consult. Case C uses Dr. Lee for consult/surgery. Note: Dr. Lee sees 45M and 28M. This is possible as he works in Surgery but consults in ER or Surgery. Sequence: The timestamps are strictly increasing within each inferred case, and the activities follow a logical medical workflow (Register -> Triage -> Treatment -> Discharge).

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Double Check: Event 1: 45M Event 2: 62F Event 3: 28M Event 4: 45M Event 5: 62F Event 6: 28M Event 7: 45M Event 8: 62F Event 9: 28M Event 10: 62F Event 11: 62F Event 12: 45M Event 13: 28M Event 14: 62F Event 15: 45M Event 16: 28M Event 17: 62F Event 18: 28M Counts: Case 1 (45M): 1, 4, 7, 12, 15 (5 events) Case 2 (62F): 2, 5, 8, 10, 11, 14, 17 (7 events) Case 3 (28M): 3, 6, 9, 13, 16, 18 (6 events) Total: 5 + 7 + 6 = 18. All events accounted for. Workflow Logic Check: Case 1 (45M): Register -> Triage -> X-ray -> Consult -> Discharge. (Makes sense for fracture). Case 2 (62F): Register -> Triage -> Lab Request -> Lab Complete -> Consult -> Rx -> Discharge. (Makes sense for infection). Case 3 (28M): Register -> Consult -> Prep -> Surgery -> Recovery -> Discharge. (Makes sense for scheduled surgery). Everything aligns perfectly.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Event 12 says Doctor consultation, resource: Dr. Lee, department: ER. Event 6 says Doctor consultation, resource: Dr. Lee, department: Surgery. This confirms Dr. Lee sees both patients (Case 1 and Case 3). This doesnt invalidate the grouping, but its worth noting that resource overlap doesnt override demographic consistency. Event 15: Patient discharged, resource: Ward Clerk, department: ER. Event 17: Patient discharged, resource: Ward Clerk, department: ER. Event 18: Patient discharged, resource: Ward Clerk, department: Surgery. This aligns with the departments. Case 1 & 2 are ER patients. Case 3 is Surgery patient.
